summary:
There is no consistent documentation indicating which particular finger has both its dorsal interosseous muscles bilaterally attached; typical anatomy shows variability without specifying such an attachment pattern for fingers Little through Ring individually and universally across populations studied so far.
The analysis did not find definitive information linking exactly how many or specifically where these muscle pairs are found bilaterally among those listed digits based solely upon current medical knowledge presented here. To provide more precise guidance would require further specialized anatomical research focusing directly on this aspect. Agent1 reasoned by searching for documented instances of double dorsal interossei attaching exclusively to single specified fingers but concluded due to lack thereof, standard human hand anatomy allows natural variance making it impossible pinpoint exact location from provided choices alone. No conclusive answer was reached regarding whether index, middle ring little had distinct dual connections as per initial query posed about them having 'two' dorsals interconnected at same spot versus usual singular pairings seen normally between adjacent phalanges within each respective metacarpophalangeal joint region during routine musculoskeletal assessments performed clinically today according
